rbaHistory.note: First cut since Nov 2020 — CPI back in 2-3% band
Held; trimmed mean CPI above target
Held; Q1 CPI surprised to upside

news.title: ASX edges higher as Virgin soars weathering the fuel shock, oil stocks fall
news.snippet: The Australian sharemarket notched up another small gain on Wednesday amid optimism the US and Iran will meet for a second round of talks to end their war.

news.title: ASX set for flat open, Wall Street eyes record as rally continues; $A stronger
news.snippet: US stocks are flirting with a record following their big rally the last two weeks on hopes that the global economy can avoid a worst-case scenario because of the US-Iran war.

news.title: Navigating turbulent times, airlines look to apps to keep travellers happy
news.snippet: Airlines now use AI-powered tools to explain flight delays and offer automatic rebooking for cancellations. Apps are becoming an increasingly important feature in improving their customer service.

news.title: Another interest rate rise will tip Australia into a recession we don’t have to have
news.snippet: Higher fuel prices mean that households who have little choice about how much fuel they use have less to spend on other goods and services, which reduces demand in much the same way as higher interest rates do.

news.title: Workplace safety regulator investigating two suicides among NAB staff
news.snippet: The federal workplace health and safety authority is investigating the circumstances surrounding two NAB employees who died by suicide just weeks apart.

news.title: Union’s move on fuel vouchers as apprentices feel the pinch
news.snippet: Thousands of workers will be eligible for $100 fuel vouchers, as cost of living pressures put Australia’s housing future at risk.

news.title: ASX to gain on oil price drop
news.snippet: The prospect of the US and Iran meeting for more peace talks has taken some sting out of oil prices, pushing ASX futures toward gains.

news.title: RBA worker loses major wage claim
news.snippet: An RBA employee who was medically unfit to work consecutive days or take on any “high stress” activities has made a failed wage claim to Fair Work.

news.title: Iran holds the trump card in this energy crisis
news.snippet: A peace deal would have to be on terms closer to Iran’s demands than to Trump’s surrender ultimatum. We are a long way from that awful moment of realism in the Oval Office.

news.title: ‘Faster’: Fertiliser fix amid food fears
news.snippet: Labor is making it easier to bring in foreign fertiliser amid fears growers will not have enough for April seeding.

news.title: Finance giant hit with $4m spam fine
news.snippet: An Aussie finance giant has copped a massive fine after breaching spam regulations millions of times – despite being fined for similar conduct years before.

news.title: ‘Plagiarised’: Big call on shock policy
news.snippet: A core claim underpinning the Coalition’s migration plan has been rebuked by the Home Affairs Minister.
